cat_2: He calls the cat a 'real friend' after the kids tell him he can't play basketball. I know that going through a hard time together, like being bullied and left out, can make you bond strongly with an animal.
cat_3: You should choose true friends who care about you over kids who just want to make fun of people.
cat_4: The kids in the story laughed at the boy and the wet cat, but the cat was actually there for him when he was crying. Real friends in life stick by you when you're sad, instead of bullying you for being different.
lying_1: She doesn't tell the teacher because Rachel reminds her that they are best friends. In real life, telling on your best friend can ruin your friendship, so she stays quiet to avoid losing her friend.
lying_2: The comic shows Rachel whispering 'I'm your best friend' when Sarah catches her. I know that at school, people hate snitches, so Sarah probably felt pressured to keep the secret so they wouldn't fight.
lying_3: A lesson is that you shouldn't put your friends in a bad situation by cheating. It forces them to choose between doing the right thing and being loyal to you.
lying_4: Rachel uses their friendship as an excuse to make Sarah cover up her cheating. True friends in real life don't force you to hide their bad choices and risk getting in trouble yourself.
stealing_1: He kept the wallet because it had exactly $300, which is the exact price of the red bike he wanted. He let his selfish desire for the bike override his morals.
stealing_2: The comic shows him holding the $300 and thinking about the red bike. I think when people want something really badly and get told no, they sometimes ignore the fact that stealing is wrong just to get it.
stealing_3: You shouldn't let your anger make you do bad things like stealing. Just because you can't get what you want doesn't mean it's okay to take from others.

lying_1: Sarah doesn't tell the teacher because Rachel reminded her that they are best friends. Friends usually protect each other and don't want to get their friend in trouble or ruin the friendship.
lying_2: The story says they've been best friends since they were toddlers and Rachel whispers not to tell on her. I know from my own life that nobody wants to be a snitch on their best friend because it feels like a betrayal.
lying_3: A good lesson is that true friends shouldn't put you in a bad situation where you have to lie and cover up for their bad choices.
lying_4: Rachel made Sarah lie to Ms. Craig just so she wouldn't get a zero on the test. In the real world, fake friends will use the 'best friend' card to peer pressure you into doing something wrong.
stealing_1: He kept the wallet because it had exactly 300 dollars in it, which was the exact amount he needed to buy the beautiful red bike his dad refused to get him.
stealing_2: In the text Billy literally says 'With that money, I could buy that red bike!' Plus, I know that when kids really want a toy but their parents say no, they can get desperate and temptation makes them steal.
stealing_3: Just because you can't have something you really want doesn't mean you should steal from innocent people.
stealing_4: Billy was mad at his parents, but he ended up taking a lady's wallet and lying to her face. It shows that in real life, letting your anger and greed control you over a material thing just hurts other people.

cat_1: Bruno kept the cat because the cat actually comforted him when he was crying, while the other boys just bullied him and the cat.
cat_2: The story says the cat tried to cheer him up when he was sad, but the boys made fun of him and said he couldn't play if he touched the dirty cat. True friendship is based on caring for each other, not peer pressure, so Bruno chose the cat who was actually there for him.
cat_3: Doing the right thing and being kind is more important than trying to act cool to fit in with bullies.
cat_4: Bruno gave up playing basketball with the boys to take the freezing cat home. People who give you ultimatums to be mean to innocent animals aren't real friends anyway.
lying_1: Sarah didn't tell on Rachel because they have been best friends since they were little toddlers and she didn't want to get her in trouble.
lying_2: The text says Rachel whispered 'Don't tell on me. I'm your best friend.' When you have a really close friend in real life, you usually feel loyal to them and it's super awkward to snitch on them to a teacher.
lying_3: It is wrong to use your friendship to guilt someone into covering up your own bad choices.
lying_4: Rachel decided not to study and cheated, and then she used her friendship to make Sarah lie to Ms. Craig. It's not cool to trap your friends into lying for you just because you were lazy.
stealing_1: He kept the lady's wallet because it had exactly $300 in it, which was the exact amount he needed to buy that beautiful bike he saw earlier.
stealing_2: The story says his dad said no to buying him the bike, and then he saw $300 in the lady's wallet. In real life, when people get rejected for something they are obsessed with, they sometimes make bad choices and steal when they see easy money.
stealing_3: You shouldn't let your obsession with material things cause you to steal from innocent people.
stealing_4: Billy was so mad about his parents not getting him the bike that he lied to the lady and kept her money. Stealing to get what you want just hurts others who probably worked hard for their money.

cat_1: He kept the cat because he knew exactly what it felt like to be laughed at by the other kids. When he saw the cat shivering in the rain, he felt empathy for it and decided to give it a real home.
cat_2: In the comic, the boy was crying on the bench and the kids made fun of him, and then they did the same exact thing to the cat. I know that going through hard times yourself can make you care more about animals who are also having a hard time.
cat_3: You should do the right thing and be kind, even if your friends are being mean or might make fun of you for it.
cat_4: The boy told the other kids he didn't care if they wouldn't let him play basketball, and he pet the cat anyway. Real courage is standing up to peer pressure to help someone who needs it.

lying_4: Rachel didn't study at all and then used Sarah to get away with cheating. In life, good friends should do the right thing themselves instead of making you feel guilty and forcing you to cover for their own mistakes.

stealing_3: You shouldn't steal from people just to get what you want, because it is selfish and really hurts others.
stealing_4: Billy lied right to the lady's face while she was upset looking for her wallet. In the real world, stealing someone's money causes them a lot of stress just so you can have a toy you wanted.

stealing_2: The story says his dad told him no to the bike, so when Billy saw the $300 he immediately thought about buying it. Sometimes when kids are mad and really want something, they justify taking it instead of working to earn the money honestly.
stealing_3: Don't let jealousy and anger make you do dishonest things, especially when you already have enough.
